Container.Name: NanoClaw-AIO
Container.Overview:
NanoClaw is a lightweight alternative to OpenClaw that runs in containers for security. Connects to WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Gmail and other messaging apps, has memory, scheduled jobs, and runs directly on Anthropic's Agents SDK.
[b]All-In-One (AIO) Edition[/b] This template automatically handles the Docker-in-Docker setup and Node.js bootstrapping required for NanoClaw. Simply provide your API keys and the container will self-configure.
[b]Initial Setup:[/b] 1. Provide your Anthropic API Key. 2. Provide your chosen messaging app tokens (e.g. Telegram Bot Token). 3. Start the container and check the logs for initialization status.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: Your Anthropic API key to run Claude models.

Config.Name: Assistant Name
Config.Description: The name your AI will respond to.

Config.Name: App Volumes - Workspace
Config.Description: Persistent storage for NanoClaw's SQLite database, configuration, and agent memory.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required to spawn isolated agent containers. (Note: Ensure your Unraid setup permits this safely).

Config.Name: [Telegram] Bot Token
Config.Description: Your Telegram bot token from @BotFather. Required if you want to use Telegram.

Config.Name: [Telegram] Chat ID
Config.Description: Your personal Chat ID or Group ID on Telegram to bind the agent to.

Config.Name: Auto-Setup Telegram
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to have the startup script automatically execute '/add-telegram' and register the chat using the provided Token and ID.
